mnemonics: Make Sure Every Process Is Relevant, Not Under The Radar
Men And Women Keep Producing Innovative Results
keyPoints: Create a Process (CPP): Create a new process by creating a new image, which includes a new program counter, register values, and stack information.
Destroy a Process (DPP): Destroy a process by killing it, which releases all its resources, and frees up the resources back to the system.
Read/Write a Process (RWP): Read/Write a process by accessing its memory space, which involves reading/writing data to its registers, stack, and memory.
Execute a Process (EP): Execute a process by executing the instructions in its program, which involves updating the program counter, register values, and stack information.
Stop/Resume a Process (SRP): Stop/Resume a process by pausing its execution, which involves updating the program counter, register values, and stack information.
Wait/Interrupt a Process (WIP): Wait/Interrupt a process by waiting for it to finish, or interrupting it, which involves updating the program counter, register values, and stack information.
